what_it_does: Defines a two-faced, status-tagged completion checklist for the live guided right-hand-man review surface, grouping criteria A–V, adding dependency ordering, coverage-round appendices, re-scope ownership notes, and found-in-use challenges.
observations: Every criterion has both a FUNCTION (behavior verified by real use) and a FORM (operator-facing shape verified by design rubric/render).
Status marks are split honestly: ✅ verified-by-use, 🟡 code-complete-untested, 🔴 needs-tim/not-built.
Each criterion carries a reuse label: READY, MODERATE, or NET-NEW.
Original groups A–I cover show-me sequence, talk-back, comprehension, addressed markup, batch generate, mockup-aware stops, temporal deixis, voice-in, mode integration.
Coverage rounds append groups J–N (vault organ merge, cognition cast, substrate one-liners, coherence oracle, refinements).

Found-in-use groups P–V append runtime challenges discovered while building: occlusion bugs, store mismatch for generate, element deixis, model visibility, FORM gaps, verification hygiene, and element-addressing chain.
Settled decisions are baked in: simple consent (one approve + git revert), comprehension verified working, temporal deixis scoped, voice-in included, approve_reach dropped.
Priority order is dependency-based, not a schedule, with B1 streaming and F2 generate-for-mockups as make-or-break gates.
Re-scope notes split ownership across cognition, coherence, and the operator-surface lane.
Standing rules include verify by real interaction (not DOM presence) and browser/process hygiene.
standouts: F2 · GENERATE-FOR-MOCKUPS is labeled the make-or-break founding promise of the surface.
Q1 · comment → generate store mismatch is flagged as the critical found-in-use break.
V-B · runtime element comprehension for unregistered elements is the immediate fix after a registry-is-truth violation.
I5 · anti-parallel-system guard is a cross-cutting invariant against duplicating subsystems.
P1/P2 · occlusion/verify-on-top bugs are elevated from one-off fixes to a class of criterion.
conventions: Two-faced criteria: FUNCTION + FORM required for every item.
Status emojis with explicit honest meanings and a rule against green-painting.
Reuse labels READY/MODERATE/NET-NEW on each criterion.
Cross-references (e.g., cross-ref A3, N·I3) instead of duplicating criteria.
Coverage rounds append new groups rather than filtering threads out.
Forward-notes for low-urgency/deferred threads so nothing is silently dropped.
Re-scope sections mark criteria as CONSUME/DECLARE/HANDED/MINE to avoid rebuilds.
Found-in-use challenges are appended as real two-faced criteria per grow-scope law.
concerns: The document is very long and has been re-scoped multiple times, making ownership and current status hard to track at a glance.
Many items remain 🔴 not-built or 🟡 untested, so the file is more of a living backlog than a done-list.
Group V (element addressing) is expansive and cross-lane, risking scope growth before core loops are proven.
Some statuses are targets rather than measured states, which could be mistaken for progress.
The accumulation of found-in-use groups could obscure the original priority order.
notes: A sprawling, two-faced completion ledger for the guided review surface that keeps absorbing discovered build challenges into new criteria groups.
questions: What is the exact current code state for each criterion versus the claims made?
Which criteria are now owned by which lane after the 2026-06-09 three-fork split?
How were the 'verified-by-use' claims actually exercised and recorded?
Is the `mockup://` address scheme registered yet, or is it still a one-line prerequisite?
Was M3's tracked pre-commit hook unified into coherence's shared gate suite or kept separately?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a truth-table/bar for done; actual content is a living, growing scope ledger with dependency ordering, coverage appendices, and re-scope notes — aligned but far more expansive.
apparent_themes: No code-reading as verification; real use only.
Simple consent and no per-address consent tiers.
Grow-scope law: every challenge becomes a criterion.
Unification of the human-interaction organ across consumers.
intention_signals: To enforce honest, use-verified completion for a human-in-the-loop review surface while preventing parallel systems, green-painted status, and unowned scope — and to turn every runtime challenge into a tracked, two-faced criterion.
novelty: The explicit FUNCTION/FORM two-faced criterion shape with a 'needs-tim' non-green-paint rule; the anti-parallel-system guard; and the practice of appending found-in-use challenges as first-class completion criteria rather than one-off bug notes.
contribution: A rigorous completion-rubric pattern that pairs behavior verification with operator-facing form, plus a scope-absorption mechanism that converts discovered build problems into tracked criteria — useful as a template for honest verification and scope management.
summary_for_embedding: Completion Criteria.md is a two-faced, status-tagged checklist defining what must be true for the company's guided right-hand-man review surface, organized into groups A–V with dependency ordering and re-scope notes.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an honest, use-verified bar for done that prevents green-painting and absorbs discovered build challenges as first-class criteria.
